message:
fix(sync): restore pre-recovery backup on ANY sync-subcommand failure, not just --import-only

Commit 2e8a3ce added transactional restore of the pre-recovery
backup when the explicit-import branch failed. That fixed the
common path (`br sync --import-only --rename-prefix --force`) but
left two other subcommands with a latent hole: if the deferred-
recovery path handed back a fresh empty DB and then `execute_flush`
OR `execute_merge` failed against that fresh DB, the backup was
never restored — the user was left staring at an empty DB with
their real data moved aside in recovery_dir/.

Fix: lift the backup-restore logic out of the import branch and
wrap the whole sync subcommand dispatch in a closure so it can
return `Result<(), BeadsError>` back to one common match arm:

  let command_result = (|| {
      // status / flush / merge / import dispatch
      ...
  })();

  match command_result {
      Ok(()) => discard_pending_recovery_backup(),
      Err(command_err) => restore_pending_recovery_backup() else
                         wrap both errors in WithContext,
  }

The diagnostic message adjusts from "explicit import failed..." to
"sync command failed..." so the error text is accurate for the
wider scope. recovery_dir is still included when available so the
operator sees exactly where the backup lives.

No-op for `--status` (read-only, never restores — a `return` from
inside the closure short-circuits through the same Ok arm).

tests/e2e_errors.rs
-------------------
The existing test
`e2e_sync_rename_prefix_applies_after_missing_db_recovery_with_
force` already exercises the success path. Adds two new cases:
  - `e2e_sync_merge_failure_restores_pre_recovery_backup`:
    deferred-recovery fresh DB, then `execute_merge` fails on a
    malformed merge source, asserts the original DB family is
    restored and next `br list` shows the pre-recovery issues.
  - `e2e_sync_flush_only_failure_restores_pre_recovery_backup`:
    same shape but drives the failure through `execute_flush` with
    a write-protected JSONL path.
Both cases assert the restored DB's row count matches the original
fixture and the recovery_dir is empty after restore (backup moved
back in place, not left orphaned).

Cargo.lock left uncommitted — local path-override artifact.
